title: MachinePart Object
description: Object representing some part of a machine, such as the static base of the machine, an axis, or the attachment points for tools and fixtures.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Delete this part and its children from the kinematics tree.

properties.name: axis
properties.description: Get the axis object for this part used to reference this part for other operations. Only valid when partType is Axis, otherwise returns null

properties.name: children
properties.description: Get the collection of child parts.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Get the internal ID of the part. This is unique with respect to other MachineParts in the Machine.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parent
properties.description: Get or set the parent of this part. Returns null if this part is a root part. Setting the parent will add this part to the end of the parent's children collection. Setting the parent will throw an error if the new parent is this part or a child of this part.

properties.name: partType
properties.description: Get the type of this part.

properties.name: spindle
properties.description: Get the spindle object for this part used to reference this part for other operations. Will return null if the part has no spindle assigned.

properties.name: toolStation
properties.description: Get the tool station object for this part. Will return null if the part has no tool station assigned.

axis.title: MachinePart.axis Property
axis.description: Get the axis object for this part used to reference this part for other operations. Only valid when partType is Axis, otherwise returns null

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machinePart_var.axis

objectType.title: MachinePart.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machinePart_var.objectType

parent.title: MachinePart.parent Property
parent.description: Get or set the parent of this part. Returns null if this part is a root part. Setting the parent will add this part to the end of the parent's children collection. Setting the parent will throw an error if the new parent is this part or a child of this part.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machinePart_var.parent

# Set the value of the property.
machinePart_var.parent = propertyValue

spindle.description: Get the spindle object for this part used to reference this part for other operations. Will return null if the part has no spindle assigned.

toolStation.description: Get the tool station object for this part. Will return null if the part has no tool station assigned.

classType.title: MachinePart.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: MachinePart.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Delete this part and its children from the kinematics tree.
